title: On Ukrainian Front, Grinding War and Weary Anticipation of Invasion
content: After eight years in the trenches, Ukrainian soldiers are resigned to the possibility that the Russian military, which dwarfs their own in power and wealth, will come sooner or later.
en_title: On Ukrainian Front, Grinding War and Weary Anticipation of Invasion
en_content: After eight years in the trenches, Ukrainian soldiers are resigned to the possibility that the Russian military, which dwarfs their own in power and wealth, will come sooner or later.

title: Aung San Suu Kyi Falls, but Myanmar’s Democratic Hopes Move On
content: The ousted civilian leader faces years in custody after being sentenced on the first of several charges. In her absence, a new generation of younger, more progressive politicians is emerging.

more_info.byline: By Sui-Lee Wee and Richard C. Paddock
des_facet: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat

en_title: Aung San Suu Kyi Falls, but Myanmar’s Democratic Hopes Move On
en_content: The ousted civilian leader faces years in custody after being sentenced on the first of several charges. In her absence, a new generation of younger, more progressive politicians is emerging.

title: Three of a Group of Missionaries Kidnapped in Haiti Have Been Released
content: A U.S. Christian aid group said three more people were released of the 17 who had been kidnapped by a gang in Haiti. Two were released last month.

more_info.byline: By Oscar Lopez and Maria Abi-Habib

en_title: Three of a Group of Missionaries Kidnapped in Haiti Have Been Released
en_content: A U.S. Christian aid group said three more people were released of the 17 who had been kidnapped by a gang in Haiti. Two were released last month.

title: What’s Driving Putin’s Ukraine Brinkmanship?
content: There are tactical reasons for threatening an invasion, but the real cause may lie in the Kremlin’s fixation with righting what it sees as a historical injustice.
en_title: What’s Driving Putin’s Ukraine Brinkmanship?
en_content: There are tactical reasons for threatening an invasion, but the real cause may lie in the Kremlin’s fixation with righting what it sees as a historical injustice.

title: India and Russia Expand Defense Ties, Despite Prospect of U.S. Sanctions
content: India’s purchase of a missile defense system signaled that it was more worried about an emboldened China at its borders than about angering the United States.

more_info.byline: By Mujib Mashal and Karan Deep Singh

en_title: India and Russia Expand Defense Ties, Despite Prospect of U.S. Sanctions
en_content: India’s purchase of a missile defense system signaled that it was more worried about an emboldened China at its borders than about angering the United States.

title: 8 Women and 8 Men: Germany Gets Its First Gender-Equal Cabinet
content: Angela Merkel is handing over the chancellery to a man. But in a watershed moment for the country, women will run all security and diplomacy-related ministries in the new government.
en_title: 8 Women and 8 Men: Germany Gets Its First Gender-Equal Cabinet
en_content: Angela Merkel is handing over the chancellery to a man. But in a watershed moment for the country, women will run all security and diplomacy-related ministries in the new government.

title: Companies Linked to Russian Ransomware Hide in Plain Sight
content: Cybersecurity experts tracing money paid by American businesses to Russian ransomware gangs found it led to one of Moscow’s most prestigious addresses.
en_title: Companies Linked to Russian Ransomware Hide in Plain Sight
en_content: Cybersecurity experts tracing money paid by American businesses to Russian ransomware gangs found it led to one of Moscow’s most prestigious addresses.

title: In Its War on Covid-19, China Calls on ‘Little Inoculated Warriors’
content: The country regards children as crucial in its quest for herd immunity, but some parents, worried about the vaccines’ safety, are pushing back.

en_title: In Its War on Covid-19, China Calls on ‘Little Inoculated Warriors’
en_content: The country regards children as crucial in its quest for herd immunity, but some parents, worried about the vaccines’ safety, are pushing back.

title: Scandal-Shaken Austria Gets Its 3rd Chancellor This Year
content: Karl Nehammer, the former interior minister, becomes the country’s leader two months after the resignation of Sebastian Kurz, amid an investigation into corruption and influence-peddling.

more_info.byline: By Isabella Kwai and Christopher F. Schuetze

en_title: Scandal-Shaken Austria Gets Its 3rd Chancellor This Year
en_content: Karl Nehammer, the former interior minister, becomes the country’s leader two months after the resignation of Sebastian Kurz, amid an investigation into corruption and influence-peddling.

title: Spike in Omicron Variant Cases Puts Europe on Edge
content: With cases of the Omicron variant rising in Europe, there are worries that even tougher restrictions are looming over a holiday period that many had hoped would be a return to some normalcy.

en_title: Spike in Omicron Variant Cases Puts Europe on Edge

title: When the Biggest Spenders Aren’t Coming Back Any Time Soon
content: Even before Omicron’s arrival, China was discouraging its citizens from traveling abroad. That has had a huge impact on global tourism.

title: Fatalities Reported After Military Truck Rams Protesters in Myanmar
content: Witnesses said soldiers also fired into the crowd and kicked wounded demonstrators, the latest in a series of confrontations in which the military’s behavior has infuriated citizens.

title: With its private-sector vaccine mandate, N.Y.C. has gone where few other U.S. cities and counties have.
content: Most local vaccine mandates in the U.S. have been limited to public-sector workers and certain other groups like health care workers or teachers.

title: Stricter coronavirus testing rules take effect for travelers flying to the U.S.
content: Inbound travelers must now show a negative result from a test taken no more than a day before departure, a requirement some say may be hard to satisfy.

more_info.byline: By Juston Jones and Jenny Gross

en_title: Stricter coronavirus testing rules take effect for travelers flying to the U.S.
en_content: Inbound travelers must now show a negative result from a test taken no more than a day before departure, a requirement some say may be hard to satisfy.

title: Pope Exploring a 2nd Meeting With Russian Orthodox Church
content: Speaking after his trip to Cyprus and Greece, Francis also said he had no choice but to accept the resignation of the archbishop of Paris because of the harmful gossip surrounding him.

title: New York City sets a sweeping vaccine mandate for all private employers.
content: Mayor Bill de Blasio described the measure as a “pre-emptive strike” designed to stall another wave of coronavirus cases and help reduce transmission of the Omicron variant.

title: Thailand reports its first case of the Omicron variant, in a U.S. citizen visiting from Spain.
content: A U.S. citizen who entered the country nearly a week ago received his first positive test result on Dec. 1. Thailand has pushed to reopen to tourists, aiming to revive its economy.

title: Bearing Witness to Svalbard’s Fragile Splendor
content: To visitors, the Norwegian archipelago can seem both ethereal and eternal. But climate change all but guarantees an eventual collapse of its vulnerable ecosystem.
en_title: Bearing Witness to Svalbard’s Fragile Splendor
en_content: To visitors, the Norwegian archipelago can seem both ethereal and eternal. But climate change all but guarantees an eventual collapse of its vulnerable ecosystem.

title: The Pandemic Has Your Blood Pressure Rising? You’re Not Alone.
content: Average blood pressure readings increased as the coronavirus spread, new research suggests. The finding portends medical repercussions far beyond Covid-19.
en_title: The Pandemic Has Your Blood Pressure Rising? You’re Not Alone.

content: He rode a wave of royalist sentiment to win a United Nations-sponsored election in 1993, but was later ousted in a power struggle with his co-prime minister.

title: Anger Spreads in Northeastern India After Security Forces Kill 14 Civilians
content: Eight mine workers were shot in a mistaken ambush by soldiers seeking insurgents, and six protesters died later in clashes with government forces, stoking fears of further violence in the restive region.

title: Britain, a leader in genomic testing, has detected around 160 Omicron cases and tightens restrictions.
content: An additional testing requirement for travelers is being imposed, as well as a ban on visitors from Nigeria, where the variant was detected this week.

title: Merkel makes a final appeal for Germans to get vaccinated, citing the bitterness of avoidable deaths.
content: ‘Every shot helps,’ the chancellor said, days before she leaves office.

title: More than 40,000 march in Vienna against Austria’s strict coronavirus measures.
content: Protesters gathered for a second weekend of mass demonstrations against the country’s tough lockdown and a coming vaccine mandate.

title: Spy Tool Was Deployed in State-Sponsored Hack of Ugandans
content: Two journalists and one politician said they received alerts warning them of “state-sponsored” attacks on their iPhones. At least one of those attacks was linked to the powerful Israeli cyberespionage tool, Pegasus.

en_content: Two journalists and one politician said they received alerts warning them of “state-sponsored” attacks on their iPhones. At least one of those attacks was linked to the powerful Israeli cyberespionage tool, Pegasus.

title: Friends who went to an anime convention with a man who tested positive for Omicron also got sick, he says.
content: Another case is reported in Connecticut that may be tied to the convention.
